keynotes.text: Suicidal insanity, broods constantly over imaginary troubles (Aur.). Simple hypertrophy of heart. For restoring a heart damaged by acute inflammation, or from relief of sufferings of chronic hypertrophy and valvular lesions. Irritating, dry, sympathetic cough in the acute stage of rheumatic carditis, or chronic organic lesions (Spon.). Threatened paralysis of heart, post-diphtheritic. Pulse irregular in force, but regular in rhythm. Inability to speak with choking, nervous, chronic palpitation, especially after public speaking; pain < by carriage riding or lying on side. Severe stitching pain in region of heart.
Relations. - Compare: Ars., Cac., Crot., Lach., Myg., Spig.

color="#800000">NAJA TRIPUDIANS Virus of the Cobra Naja produces a typical bulbar paralysis (L. J. Boyd). Causes no hæmorrhage but only œdema, hence the victims of this reptile frequently bear very little sign of external injury a small scratch or puncture being the only indication where the fangs have worked their havoc. The tissue lying beneath the wound is colored dark purple, and a large quantity of viscid blood-like fluid collects in the vicinity of the wound. An intense burning pain at the spot bitten is the first symptom. In man there follows an interval before fresh symptoms occur. The average is about an hour. Once developed, the symptoms follow a rapid course. A feeling of intoxication is produced, followed by a loss of power over the limbs. The patient is bereft of speech, swallowing, and the control over the movement of the lips. The saliva is ejected in large quantities, the respiration gradually becomes slower and slower, and at length ceases. Conscious all time. Is not a hæmorrhagic or septic, medicine like Lachesis and Crotalus. Its action settles around the heart; valvular troubles. Marked surging of blood upwards, marked dyspnœa, inability to lie on left side. Hypertrophy, and valvular lesions. Organs seem to be drawn together. Very susceptible to cold. With heart symptoms, pain in forehead and temples. Diseases, primarily depending upon degeneration of motor cells. Control of sphincters lost.

Here is a blood relative of Lachesis, if serpent poisons may be called relatives, and according to the symptoms arising from the bite of the serpent it ought to be equally valuable as a curative, but it has not yet been found so. Why not? on referring to Allen's Encyclopædia we find twenty-nine provers, poisonings and all, for Lachesis, and forty-five for Naja. Of course, Lachesis has been longest in use; but has the difference in time been sufficient to account for the very great difference in utility? Another thing is noticeable; the provings of Lachesis were made mostly with potencies as high as the 30th, while those of Naja are almost all with the lowest preparation or the crude poison from the bite of the serpent. Does this account for it?

We also notice on referring to the same authority that all the most marked verifications are of symptoms produced by the provings of the 30th of Lachesis. Does this indicate that Naja must be proven in the potencies to develop its most efficient powers? Naja has been found of very decided use in affections of the heart, especially weak heart (Nux vomica, tired feeling); diphtheria, where there is impending heart failure or paralysis. Dyspnœa and prostration from weak heart, sympathetic cough in organic diseases with weak heart action. (Dry cough, sympathetic in heart affections, Spongia.) Palpitation and bad feeling in heart, Naja. Constantly dwells on suicide like Aurum. But further than this I do not know of very many marked successes from its use. Nevertheless, I feel convinced that with further proving and investigation, along the same line as Lachesis, it may rival if not outshine it.

mental_emotional.text: Broods constantly over imaginary troubles. Suicidal insanity (Aur). Depressed. Aversion to talking. Blurred speech. Melancholy. Dreads to be left alone. Fear of rain

Easily excited. Great depression of spirits, with temporo-frontal headache, spinal pain and palpitation of heart. Melancholy ; began to form images of possible wrongs and misfortunes over which the mind broods ; very wretched at times. Suicidal insanity, broods constantly over imaginary troubles ; sleep full of frightful dreams, and wakes with dull pain in head and fluttering of heart ; uneasy dryness of fauces ; grasping of throat, with sensation of choking and lividity of face. θ Melancholia. Paroxysms, twice a day, of delirium, incessant talking, seeing visions, imagines she is in a terrible rainstorm, complains of being so cold, and that the carriage has turned over and hurt her head. θ Cerebro-spinal meningitis. Dragging and anxiety at precordia, occurring in great grief ; also smarting pains in back from same cause.

head.text: Pain in left temple and in left orbital region, extending to occiput, with nausea and vomiting. Hay-fever, with dry larynx. Suffocative spells after sleeping (Lach). Eyes staring. Ptosis of both lids

Heaviness of eyes, with dryness of throat. Temporo-frontal headache, depression of spirits ; spinal pain ; palpitation of heart. Congestive headache dependent upon organic disease of heart. Waking with dull pain in head, attended by fluttering of heart. Weight and pressure at vertex, with cold feet and flushes of face. Pain in head, ringing in ears, palpitation, small, quick, nervous pulse ; constipation ; abdomen large ; urine scanty and sedimentitious. θ Nervous headache. Neuralgic pain in head, preceded or followed by nausea or vomiting ; pain severe in left orbital region, extended back to occiput ; aching around orbit, increased to throbbing, and thence it was drawing in character to back of head ; from overeating, mental or physical exertion. Since cessation of catamenia eight years ago, subject to agonizing pain in head, depriving her of sense and memory ; pain comes on at noon ; pain in upper part of back, and flushes of face ; has not been more than two or three days free from an attack for some years ; sleep profound ; pulse regular and small. Awakes with headache every morning ; dull, heavy constriction across forehead ; eyes sunken and dark ; incapacity for much exertion of mind or body ; headache sometimes wears off, at other times continues all day ; constant relaxed sore throat, dryness of fauces, hoarseness ; very susceptible to cold ; frequent emissions. θ Chronic headache. Dull shoots up occiput ; tired feeling in cervical and dorsal vertebræ, with peculiar burning of exhaustion ; spinal irritation.

ears.text: Illusions of hearing; otalgia; chronic otorrhœa, black discharges; smells like herring brine

respiratory.text: Grasping at throat, with sense of choking. Irritating, dry cough, dependent on cardiac lesions (Spong; Lauroc) Sticky mucus and saliva. Asthmatic constriction in evening. Asthma beginning with coryza

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Naja]. Constrictive irritation of larynx, giving rise to coughing. Spasmodic croup ; clogging up of larynx and trachea with thick mucus, which is hawked up with difficulty. Sensation of rawness in larynx and trachea, or as if there was a hair in it, causing constant tickling, coughing and hoarseness ; finally some expectoration of tenacious mucus ; breathing laborious ; gasping for breath for several hours. Diphtheria invading larynx ; impending paralysis of heart. Dyspnœa and prostration. θ Organic disease of heart. Short, hoarse cough, with rawness in larynx and upper part of trachea. Irritating, sympathetic cough, attending organic disease of heart. Cough of laryngeal and pulmonary phthisis.

chest_heart.text: Dragging and anxiety in præcordia. Feeling of weight on heart. Angina pains extending to nape of neck, left shoulder and arm with anxiety and fear of death. With the heart symptoms pain in forehead and temples. Pulse irregular in force. Threatened paralysis of heart, body cold, pulse slow, weak, irregular, tremulous. Acute and chronic endocarditis. Palpitation. Stitching pain in region of heart. Damaged heart after infections diseases. Marked symptoms of low tension (Elaps, Vipera)

Asthmatic constriction of chest for half an hour, ending in mucous expectoration. Feeling of depression and lowness about heart. Fluttering of heart, with headache ; pulse regular in rhythm, but irregular in force ; acute pain and sense of oppression in chest as though a hot iron had been run into it and a hundredweight put upon it ; cannot lie a moment on left side, but great relief of pain and breathing on lying on right side. Chronic nervous palpitation ; palpitation after preaching. Inability to speak, with choking, nervous, chronic palpitation ; chronic hypertrophy and valvular disease of heart ; considerable pain after riding in a carriage, extending to left scapula ; pain not affected by inspiration. θ Angina pectoris. Attacks of severe stitching pains in region of heart, during which respirations almost cease and death seems impending. θ Angina pectoris. Severe pains in left temple, cardiac and left ovarian regions ; great mental depression ; aversion to talking ; sensation as if heart and ovary were drawn up together ; a sense of contraction or drawing together between the organs. Pressing pain in vertex as from a weight ; shortness of breath ; palpitation of heart, < walking and lying on side ; constrictive sensation in cardiac region, as if heart was screwed together ; great anxiety ; loss of appetite ; stool normal ; slight enlargement of heart ; first sound followed by a grating, second sound accentuated ; pulse irregular, weak ; pains about heart extending to nape of neck, left shoulder and arm, with anxiety and fear of death ; attacks occur frequently during night ; pains accompanied by dry cough, particularly emanating from trachea. Irritating, sympathetic cough in acute stage of rheumatic carditis, and afterwards organic changes in valves, giving rise to tumultuous action of heart, violent, sudden throbbing, attended with endocardial murmur and increased size of organs. Simple hypertrophy of heart. For restoration of heart damaged by acute inflammation and for assuaging sufferings of chronic hypertrophy and valvular disease. Valvular disease, with loud regurgitative sounds ; pulse 60, with general anasarca. Deficient nervous influence of par vagum ; dilatation and fatty degeneration of heart. Threatening paralysis of heart. θ Diphtheria.

female.text: Neuralgia of left ovary; often serviceable in obscure pain in left groin, especially in post-operative cases; seems to be drawn to heart
Crampy pains in left ovary with severe palpitation. Ovarian congestive neuralgia, with violent palpitation. Thin, whitish leucorrhœa in afternoon.

sleep.text: Profound, like a log, with stertorous breathing, a typical reptilian state

better_from.text: from walking or riding in open air

eyes.text: Eyes fixed and staring. Passive congestion of palpebral conjunctiva ; lids look bluish and are glued together in morning.

mouth_throat.text: Great dryness of mouth. Pressure and gagging in throat. Constriction and dryness of throat. Grasping at throat, with sense of choking. Œsophagismus or spasmodic stricture of œsophagus. Soreness and pricking in left side of throat. Pharyngo-laryngeal inflammation, with dark red color of fauces and spasmodic, irritable cough. After catching cold, pain in throat and frequent tendency to short cough ; three days later, heat of skin, quick, full pulse, flushed face ; tongue white, moist and large ; r. tonsil swollen, with sharp pain in it, as from needles ; short, hard cough, < at night ; pain up right side of neck ; finally incessant cough ; considerable heat of skin ; fauces red ; larynx tender to touch, with inclination to cough when pressure was made there ; tonsils swollen. θ Acute pharyngo-laryngitis. Diphtheria in cases just like Laches., when larynx is invaded ; patient grasps at throat, with sensation of choking, fauces dark red, fetid breath, short, hoarse cough, with raw feeling in upper part of trachea. Diphtheria with impending paralysis of heart ; patient blue ; awakens from sleep gasping ; pulse intermittent and thready.

stomach_abdomen.text: Dyspepsia with foul white or yellowish white tongue ; rawness of throat ; soreness of stomach or pressive pain.

stool_rectum.text: Diarrhœa ; white, slimy, greenish stools. Constipation ; difficult, painful evacuations.

back.text: Cough, laryngismus and even spasmodic stricture of œsophagus, result of spinal irritation affecting the nucha. Pain in back, < in evening. Spinal irritation.

extremities.text: Sudden prostration of strength in limbs. Limbs swollen double natural size. θ Organic disease of heart.

generalities.text: For half an hour : asthmatic constriction of chest. For several hours : gasping for breath. Every morning : awakes with headache. For some years has not been free from an attack of pain in head for more than two or three days. Right : tonsil swollen, with pain ; pain in side of neck. Left : severe pain in orbital region ; soreness and pricking in side of throat ; crampy pains in ovary ; cannot lie upon side ; pain from heart to scapula ; severe pain in temple and ovarian regions. Pain as from needles in tonsil ; as if there was a hair in larynx ; as though a hot iron had been run into it ; as if heart and ovary were drawn up together ; pain as from a weight in vertex ; as if heart was screwed together. Pain : in head ; in spine ; in upper part of back ; in throat ; in right side of neck ; in heart ; about heart to nape of neck ; left shoulder and arm. Agonizing pain : in head. Acute pain : in chest. Severe pain : in left orbital region ; in left temple, cardiac and ovarian regions. Sharp pain : in right tonsil. Severe stitching pains : in region of heart. Neuralgic pain : in head ; in ovary. Crampy pains : in left ovary. Aching : around orbit, increased to throbbing, and thence it was drawing in character to back of head. Dull shoots : up occiput. Pressive pain : of stomach ; in vertex. Smarting pain : in back. Dull pain : in head. Peculiar burning : of exhaustion. Soreness : in left side of throat ; of stomach. Pricking : on left side of throat. Raw feeling : in upper part of trachea ; of throat ; in larynx. Depression and lowness : about heart. Dragging and anxiety : at precordia. Tired feeling : in cervical and dorsal vertebræ. Pressure : in throat. Weight and pressure : at vertex. Heaviness : over eyes. Dull, heavy constriction : across forehead. Constrictive sensation : in cardiac region. Choking sensation. Uneasy dryness : of fauces. Dryness : of throat ; of fauces ; of mouth. Acts primarily upon nervous system, especially upon respiratory nerves, the pneumogastric and glosso-pharyngeal. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Naja]. Touch : larynx tender. Riding in a carriage : considerable pain in heart. Woman, æt. 20 ; cerebrospinal meningitis. Miss, æt. 26, fair, florid, nervous, sanguine ; angina. Man, æt. 27, dark, bilious temperament ; chronic headaches. Man, æt. 32, baker, eight years ago suffered from obstinate articular rheumatism, six years ago had syphilis ; affection of heart. Woman, æt. 32, feeble, nervous, suffering several years ; neuralgia in head. Woman, æt. 35, mother of four children, has frequently aborted, brother and sister died of consumption, she herself having been homeopathically cured of the disease, a slight dyspnœa only remaining ; angina pectoris. Woman, æt. 50, suffering about a year ; irritability of heart. Woman, æt. 56, catamenia cease eight years ago, since then suffering ; headaches. Woman, æt. 60 ; headache.

_raw: Compare : Arsen., Cactus, Crotal., Iberis, Laches., Mygale, Sumbul, Spigel
